summary: Comprehensive testing of multiplayer Cold War Artillery game - Auth, Lobby, and Room features. All backend APIs pass (18/18 tests). Frontend login, registration, logout, lobby, and singleplayer mode all work correctly. Critical issue found: WebSocket authentication fails because session cookie is httponly and cannot be read by JavaScript for WS handshake.

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket connection fails with 403 error because session_token cookie is httponly and cannot be read by JavaScript. The frontend tries to read the cookie via document.cookie but fails since httponly cookies are not accessible from JS.

passed_tests: API root endpoint returns correct message
GET /api/auth/me returns 401 when not authenticated
POST /api/auth/register creates new user and sets session cookie
POST /api/auth/register returns 400 for duplicate email
POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns user data and cookie
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials returns 401
GET /api/auth/me returns user data when authenticated
POST /api/auth/logout clears session
GET /api/rooms returns 401 when not authenticated
GET /api/rooms returns list of rooms when authenticated
POST /api/rooms creates new room
POST /api/rooms returns 401 when not authenticated
POST /api/rooms/{room_id}/join adds guest to room
Cannot join own room (400 error)
Joining nonexistent room returns 404
DELETE /api/rooms/{room_id} deletes room as host
POST /api/trajectory calculates missile path
Test user (test@test.com) login works
Login page renders with email/password form and Google OAuth button
Toggle between login/register modes works
User registration via UI works and redirects to lobby
User login via UI works and redirects to lobby
Logout clears session and redirects to login
Lobby shows user greeting, Solo button, Logout button
Lobby shows available rooms list with join buttons
Lobby shows rooms in progress (Em Andamento)
Room creation form visible when user has no waiting room
Room creation form hidden when user has waiting room
Navigating to singleplayer mode works
Singleplayer game canvas renders correctly
Back button from singleplayer returns to lobby
Multiplayer game page loads with all UI elements
Multiplayer game shows canvas, scoreboard (USA/USSR), Round indicator
Chat UI renders with toggle, input, and send button
Voice chat button is present in multiplayer game
failed_tests: WebSocket connection for multiplayer game - fails with 403 due to httponly cookie issue
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix WebSocket authentication - The session_token cookie is set with httponly=True (line 80 server.py), which prevents JavaScript from reading it. The frontend (MultiplayerGame.jsx line 118) tries to read it via document.cookie which fails. Options: (1) Remove httponly flag and keep cookie accessible (less secure), OR (2) Create a separate non-httponly token for WebSocket auth, OR (3) Use a different WS auth approach like sending token via first WS message after connection
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 80: httponly=True on session cookie breaks WebSocket auth pattern used in MultiplayerGame.jsx
MultiplayerGame.jsx line 114-118: getCookie function cannot read httponly cookies - this is the root cause of WS 403 errors

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all UI flows work, WS connection fails)

seed_data_creation: Test user test@test.com with password test123 was verified working. Multiple test rooms created during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs are all working correctly. Frontend login/logout/registration/lobby/singleplayer all work. The CRITICAL issue is WebSocket authentication for multiplayer game. The session cookie is httponly so JavaScript cannot read it to pass to WebSocket connection. The fix needs to either: (1) change cookie to not be httponly, or (2) implement alternative WS auth mechanism. After fix, test WS connection in multiplayer game page.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The session_token cookie is set with httponly=True in server.py line 80. HttpOnly cookies cannot be accessed by JavaScript. The frontend (MultiplayerGame.jsx line 118) attempts to read this cookie using document.cookie.match() which returns empty string because the cookie is inaccessible. The WebSocket URL is constructed with ?token= query parameter but the token is empty, causing the server to reject the connection.
reproduction_steps: 3. Check browser console - see 'WebSocket connection failed: 403' error
4. Check browser Application tab - session_token cookie has HttpOnly flag
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Change httponly=False in make_session_cookie() function OR implement a separate endpoint to get a WS-specific token that can be read by JavaScript
